LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF SEHAT SUTARDJA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO ANSWER AND/OR FURTHER ANSWER SECOND SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND SECOND SET OF DOCUMENT REQUESTS AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR FURTHER RESPONSES TO SECOND SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND SECOND SET OF DOCUMENT REQUESTS ARE EXCESSIVE AND UNDULY BURDENSOME. IN ADDITION, MOST OF THE INTERROGATORIES WOULD NECESSITATE THE PREPARATION OR THE MAKING OF A COMPILATION, ABSTRACT, AUDIT, OR SUMMARY OF OR FROM THE DOCUMENTS OF THE PARTY TO WHOM THE INTERROGATORY IS DIRECT. (CCP 2030.230.) MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: KAREN SCOTT, CSR #4027

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND EVIDENCE FILED BY PLAINTIFF SUTARDJA, SEHAT HEARING SET FOR AUG-07-2014 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SEHAT SUTARDJA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HIS INITIAL BURDEN ON SUMMARY JUDGMENT OF SHOWING THAT THE FTB HAS ADOPTED THE "FTB STOCK OPTION REGULATION" DEFINED IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC"). THE FAC PLEADS AT PARAGRAPHS 8 AND 12 THE "GENERAL POLICIES" THAT COMPRISE THE FTB STOCK OPTION REGULATION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THAT THE FTB HAS ADOPTED AS A REGULATION THE PART OF THE FTB STOCK OPTION REGULATION DESCRIBED IN PARAGRAPH 12. THE ONLY "UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACT" ("UMF") SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFF TO ESTABLISH THAT DEFENDANT ADOPTED THE FTB STOCK OPTION REGULATION AS DEFINED IN THE FAC IS THAT DEFENDANT "ADOPTED THEOFFICE OF THE CHIEF COUNSEL INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE MEMORANDUM NUMBER 200728042 AS AN ENFORCEMENT FOR POLICY FOR SECTION 409A IN THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA." (PLAINTIFF'S UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACT NO. 8.) HOWEVER, THE IRS MEMO 200728042 CITED IN PLAINTIFF'S UMF NO. 8 DOES NOT ADDRESS THE PART OF THE FTB STOCK OPTION REGULATION PLEAD IN PARAGRAPH 12. IN ANY EVENT, THE COURT MAY NOT GRANT SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185, TEL. NO. (951)764-4047 (302/MJM)
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-04-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Evidence
Off Calendar Aug-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEFFREY A. LEON OF LEON & LEON, TEL. NO. (510) 208-6600 APPEARING FOR THE
PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. GLENN A. SMITH, TEL. NO. (650) 473-1000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARGUERITE STRICKLIN, TEL. NO. (510) 622-2146 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD;
ATTY. NATASHA SHERWOOD PAGE, TEL. NO. (916) 845-6729 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF SEHAT SUTARDJA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff has not met his initial burden on summary judgment of showing that the FTB has adopted the "FTB Stock Option Regulation" defined in the First Amended Complaint ("FAC"). The FAC pleads at paragraphs 8 and 12 the "general policies" that comprise the FTB Stock Option Regulation. Plaintiff has not presented sufficient evidence to support that the FTB has adopted as a regulation the part of the FTB Stock Option Regulation described in paragraph 12. The only "undisputed material fact" ("UMF") submitted by Plaintiff to establish that Defendant adopted the FTB Stock Option Regulation as defined in the FAC is that Defendant "adopted the ' Office of the Chief Counsel Internal Revenue Service Memorandum Number 200728042 as an enforcement for policy for Section 409A in the State of California." (Plaintiff's Undisputed Material Fact no. 8.) However, the IRS Memo 200728042 cited in Plaintiff's UMF no. 8 does not address the part of the FTB Stock Option Regulation plead in paragraph 12. In any event, the court may not grant summary judgment on part of a cause of action. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185, Tel. No. (951)764-4047 =(302/MJM)

Plaintiff Sehat Sutardja'S Motion To Compel Defendant To Answer And/Or Further Answer Second Set Of Special Interrogatories And Second Set Of Document Req.'S And Req. For Sanctions Against Defendant
Off Calendar Jan-28-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GLENN SMITH, TEL. NO. (650) 273-1000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JEFFREY A. LEON OF LEON & LEON, TEL. NO. (510) 208-6600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARGUERITE C. STRICKLIN, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. (510) 622-2146 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. NATASHA SHERWOOD PAGE, TEL. NO. (916) 845-6729 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
KAREN SCOTT OF BARKLEY COURT REPORTERS, TEL. NO. 433-5777, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Sehat Sutardja'S Motion To Compel Defendant To Answer And/Or Further Answer Second Set Of Special Interrogatories And Second Set Of Document Requests And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant is Denied. Plaintiff's requests for further responses to Second Set of Special Interrogatories and Second Set of Document Requests are excessive and unduly burdensome. In addition, most of the interrogatories would necessitate the preparation or the making of a compilation, abstract, audit, or summary of or from the documents of the party to whom the interrogatory is direct. (CCP 2030.230.) Monetary sanctions are denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Karen Scott, CSR #4027 =(302/EHG)
